title: Opatov (Svitavy District)

infobox.value: ([German](/wiki/German_language "German language"): *Abtsdorf*) is a municipality and village in [Svitavy District](/wiki/Svitavy_District "Svitavy District") in the [Pardubice Region](/wiki/Pardubice_Region "Pardubice Region") of the [Czech Republic](/wiki/Czech_Republic "Czech Republic"). It has about 1,100 inhabitants.

**Opatov** ([German](/wiki/German_language "German language"): *Abtsdorf*) is a municipality and village in [Svitavy District](/wiki/Svitavy_District "Svitavy District") in the [Pardubice Region](/wiki/Pardubice_Region "Pardubice Region") of the [Czech Republic](/wiki/Czech_Republic "Czech Republic"). It has about 1,100 inhabitants.

## Etymology

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Opatov_(Svitavy_District)&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Etymology")]

The name is derived from the Czech word *opat* ('abbot'), meaning "abbot's property". It refers to the fact that the village was initially the property of the monastery in [Litomyšl](/wiki/Litomy%C5%A1l "Litomyšl").[[2]](#cite_note-2)

## Geography

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Opatov_(Svitavy_District)&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Geography")]

Opatov is located about 8 kilometres (5 mi) north of [Svitavy](/wiki/Svitavy "Svitavy") and 56 km (35 mi) southeast of [Pardubice](/wiki/Pardubice "Pardubice"). It lies in the [Svitavy Uplands](/wiki/Svitavy_Uplands "Svitavy Uplands"). The highest point is at 550 m (1,804 ft) above sea level. The [Třebovka](/wiki/T%C5%99ebovka "Třebovka") River flows through the municipality.

The municipal territory is rich in fishponds, the largest of which are Hvězda and Nový rybník. These are important [ornithological](/wiki/Ornithology "Ornithology") sites.[[3]](#cite_note-3)

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Opatov_(Svitavy_District)&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: History")]

The first written mention of Opatov is from 1347. It was founded around 1247 by Abbot Herrman from the monastery in [Litomyšl](/wiki/Litomy%C5%A1l "Litomyšl") as a village of German settlers. In the 14th century, fishponds were established around Opatov. From 1364 until the [Thirty Years' War](/wiki/Thirty_Years%27_War "Thirty Years' War"), Opatov was a [market town](/wiki/M%C4%9Bstys "Městys"). During these wars, part of Opatov was looted and burned down. In 1771–1772, the village was hit by [famine](/wiki/Famine "Famine"). A large fire badly damaged the central part of the village in 1841. In 1846, the railway was opened.[[4]](#cite_note-history-4)

Until World War II, Germans formed majority of the population. After World War II, the [German-speaking inhabitants were expelled](/wiki/Expulsion_of_Germans_from_Czechoslovakia "Expulsion of Germans from Czechoslovakia"). The hamlets of Královec and Nový Rybník, which were administratively part of Opatov, disappeared as a result. the municipality was then partly resettled by Czechs.[[4]](#cite_note-history-4)

## Economy

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Opatov_(Svitavy_District)&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Economy")]

Opatov is known for the [Opatov Photovoltaics Plant](/wiki/Opatov_Photovoltaics_Plant "Opatov Photovoltaics Plant").

## Transport

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Opatov_(Svitavy_District)&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Transport")]

The I/43 road (from [Brno](/wiki/Brno "Brno") and [Svitavy](/wiki/Svitavy "Svitavy") to the Czech-Polish border in [Králíky](/wiki/Kr%C3%A1l%C3%ADky "Králíky")) runs through the municipality.

Opatov is located on the railway line [Česká Třebová](/wiki/%C4%8Cesk%C3%A1_T%C5%99ebov%C3%A1_railway_station "Česká Třebová railway station")–[Letovice](/wiki/Letovice "Letovice").[[7]](#cite_note-7)

## Sights

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Opatov_(Svitavy_District)&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Sights")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/d/dc/Opatov%2C_kaple_svat%C3%A9ho_Jana_Nepomuck%C3%A9ho.jpg/250px-Opatov%2C_kaple_svat%C3%A9ho_Jana_Nepomuck%C3%A9ho.jpg)](/wiki/File:Opatov,_kaple_svat%C3%A9ho_Jana_Nepomuck%C3%A9ho.jpg)

Chapel of Saint John of Nepomuk

The main landmark of Opatov is the Church of Saint Anthony the Great. It was founded in the first half of the 13th century, but was rebuilt several times. It has a massive Renaissance tower with a Romanesque core from 1240.[[4]](#cite_note-history-4)[[8]](#cite_note-8)

In the northern part of Opatov is the Chapel of Saint John of Nepomuk. It was built in the Baroque style in 1722 and is considered an artistically valuable building. In front of the chapel is a statue of Saint John of Nepomuk from 1720.[[9]](#cite_note-9)

## Twin towns – sister cities

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Opatov_(Svitavy_District)&action=edit&section=8 "Edit section: Twin towns – sister cities")]

See also: [List of twin towns and sister cities in the Czech Republic](/wiki/List_of_twin_towns_and_sister_cities_in_the_Czech_Republic "List of twin towns and sister cities in the Czech Republic")

Opatov is [twinned](/wiki/Sister_city "Sister city") with:[[10]](#cite_note-10)

* [![Italy](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/en/thumb/0/03/Flag_of_Italy.svg/40px-Flag_of_Italy.svg.png)](/wiki/Italy "Italy") [Drezzo (Colverde)](/wiki/Drezzo "Drezzo"), Italy
